message.content: acquaint

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` below begin every
7	line with exactly 1 leading space so they don't accidentally match
8	the renumber script's entry regex (`^\d+\.\s`). Minimum is the
9	principled choice — the convention's job is to dodge the
10	column-0 anchor, not to add visual emphasis. See
11	`notes/fix-todo.py`.
12	
13	## In Progress
14	
15	A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
16	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
17	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
18	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
19	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
20	
21	_No cycle currently in progress._
22	
23	## Todo
24	
25	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
26	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
27	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
28	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
29	
30	 Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is
31	 readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the
32	 entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being
33	 able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number
34	 ("let's work on #3"). Continuation lines under a two-digit
35	 entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3.
36	
37	1. **`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` subcommands.** Parallel
38	   shape to `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` (check vs rewrite).
39	   Renumber `## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries in `notes/todo.md`,
40	   normalize continuation-line indent to match prefix width.
41	   Rust replacement for the interim `notes/fix-todo.py`.
42	   Belongs in the broader "notes/repo hygiene" wave alongside
43	   `validate-repo` ([[19]]).
44	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
45	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
46	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
47	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
48	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
49	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
50	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
51	   (`init.rs`, tests).
52	
53	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
54	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
55	   issue.
56	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
57	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
58	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
59	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
60	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
61	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
62	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
63	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
64	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
65	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
66	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
67	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
68	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
69	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
70	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
71	   the felt bottleneck.
72	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
73	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
74	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
75	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
76	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
77	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
78	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
79	6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
80	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
81	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
82	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
83	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
84	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
85	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
86	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
87	7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
88	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
89	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
90	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
91	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
92	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
93	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
94	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
95	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
96	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
97	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
98	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
99	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
100	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
101	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
102	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
103	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
104	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
105	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
106	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
107	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
108	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
109	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
110	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
111	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
112	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
113	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
114	    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
115	    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
116	    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
117	    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
118	11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
119	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
120	    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
121	    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
122	    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
123	    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
124	    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
125	12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
126	    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
127	    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
128	    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
129	    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
130	13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
131	14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
132	    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
133	    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
134	    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
135	    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
136	    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
137	15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
138	    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
139	    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
140	    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
141	16. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
142	    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
143	    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
144	    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
145	    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
146	    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
147	17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
148	    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
149	    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
150	    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
151	    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
152	18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
153	    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
154	    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
155	    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
156	    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
157	19. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
158	    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
159	    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
160	    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
161	    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
162	    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
163	    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
164	20. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
165	    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
166	    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
167	    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
168	    post-init commands can. Depends on the
169	    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
170	    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
171	21. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
172	    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
173	    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
174	    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
175	    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
176	    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
177	    visual inconsistency.
178	22. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
179	    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
180	    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
181	    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
182	    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
183	23. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
184	    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
185	    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
186	    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
187	    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
188	    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
189	    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
190	    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
191	    sweep.
192	24. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
193	    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
194	    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
195	    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
196	25. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
197	    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
198	    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
199	    2026-04-24).
200	26. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
201	    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
202	    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
203	    place first. [[12]]
204	27. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
205	    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
206	    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
207	28. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
208	    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
209	    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
210	29. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
211	    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
212	    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
213	    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
214	    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
215	    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
216	    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
217	    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
218	    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
219	30. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
220	    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
221	    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
222	31. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
223	    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
224	    differs from entry. [[20]]
225	32. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
226	    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
227	    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
228	33. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
229	    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
230	    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
231	    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
232	    needed for the new layout.
233	34. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
234	    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
235	    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
236	    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
237	    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
238	35. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
239	36. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
240	37. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
241	38. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
242	39. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
243	40. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
244	41. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
245	42. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
246	43. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
247	44. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
248	    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
249	    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
250	    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
251	    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
252	    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
253	    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
254	    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
255	    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
256	    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
257	    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
258	    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
259	    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
260	    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
261	    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
262	    it adds value over the resolution chain.
263	
264	## Bugs
265	
266	   Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
267	   for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
268	   and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as
269	   `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).
270	
271	1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
272	   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
273	   four gaps:
274	   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
275	     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
276	     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
277	     are never surfaced.
278	   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
279	     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
280	     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
281	     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
282	     printed twice.
283	   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
284	     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
285	     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
286	     on write would close this.
287	   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
288	     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
289	     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
290	     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
291	     where there may be no next run.
292	
293	   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
294	   patches one related case — the detached child eating
295	   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
296	   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
297	   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
298	   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.
299	
300	## Done
301	
302	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
303	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.
304

306

308	- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
309	- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
310	- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` below begin every
line with exactly 1 leading space so they don't accidentally match
the renumber script's entry regex (`^\d+\.\s`). Minimum is the
principled choice — the convention's job is to dodge the
column-0 anchor, not to add visual emphasis. See
`notes/fix-todo.py`.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Todo

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

 Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is
 readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the
 entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being
 able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number
 ("let's work on #3"). Continuation lines under a two-digit
 entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3.

1. **`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` subcommands.** Parallel
   shape to `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` (check vs rewrite).
   Renumber `## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries in `notes/todo.md`,
   normalize continuation-line indent to match prefix width.
   Rust replacement for the interim `notes/fix-todo.py`.
   Belongs in the broader "notes/repo hygiene" wave alongside
   `validate-repo` ([[19]]).
2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[4]]
8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
16. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
19. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
20. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
    post-init commands can. Depends on the
    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
21. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
    visual inconsistency.
22. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
23. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
    sweep.
24. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
25. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
    2026-04-24).
26. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[12]]
27. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
28. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
29. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
30. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
31. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
    differs from entry. [[20]]
32. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
33. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
    needed for the new layout.
34. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
35. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
36. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
37. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
38. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
39. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
40. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
41. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
42. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
43. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
44. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.

## Bugs

   Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
   for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
   and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as
   `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).

1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.
   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
     printed twice.
   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]

1	# Architecture
2	
3	How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
4	and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
5	"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
6	per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores/chores-*.md`
7	and `notes/todo.md`.
8	
9	This is a living document; the app is under continuous
10	development and the repo will keep changing.
11	
12	## Overview
13	
14	`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
15	`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
16	with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
17	cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
18	of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
19	user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
20	workflow.
21	
22	## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params
23	
24	Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
25	operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
26	can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
27	was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
28	subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
29	the mismatch rather than fixing it.
30	
31	- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
32	  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
33	  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
34	  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
35	- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
36	  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
37	  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
38	  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
39	  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
40	    config / command-line args.
41	- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
42	  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
43	  on whether the conversion can fail.
44	
45	Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
46	subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
47	subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.
48	
49	Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
50	[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)):
51	
52	1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
53	   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
54	   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
55	2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
56	   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
57	3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
58	   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
59	   nothing to stdout itself.
60	4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
61	   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
62	   a recording sink.
63	5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
64	   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.
65	
66	None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
67	The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
68	(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
69	crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
70	in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
71	when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
72	a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.
73	
74	### Naming
75	
76	Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
77	the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:
78	
79	- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
80	  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
81	  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
82	- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
83	  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
84	  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.
85	
86	## Module map
87	
88	The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
89	catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
90	per-subcommand refactor status):
91	
92	**CLI layer:**
93	
94	- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
95	  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
96	  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
97	  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
98	  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
99	- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
100	  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
101	  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
102	  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
103	  exactly once. See
104	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
105	- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
106	  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
107	  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
108	  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
109	  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and the
110	  `-R`/`-s` resolution stack — `find_workspace_root` /
111	  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` / `resolve_repos`
112	  (the top-level entry combining the `-R PATH` override and
113	  the `-s code|bot|code,bot` role selection).
114	
115	**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**
116	
117	- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
118	  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
119	  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
120	- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
121	- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `Scope` (a newtype over
122	  `Vec<Side>` — the `code`/`bot` role set) and `parse_scope`.
123	
124	**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
125	`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
126	where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
127	`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
128	`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
129	`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
130	machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` and which
131	compose `options_flags/` leaves: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` +
132	`notes/todo.md`.
133	
134	**Support:**
135	
136	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
137	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
138	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
139	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
140	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
141	  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
142	  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).
143	
144	## Subcommand model
145	
146	Adding a subcommand `x`:
147	
148	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
149	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
150	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
151	2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
152	   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
153	   the long form).
154	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
155	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
156	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
157	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
158	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
159	   prose).
160	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
161	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
162	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
163	   and stays at this layer.
164	
165	## args → Context + Params
166	
167	"The Context+Params port" — each subcommand's
168	`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` is structured as
169	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, with an
170	`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
171	conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0)
172	is the worked example; complete across all 12 current
173	subcommands as of 0.49.0, and the shape new subcommands
174	follow. Out of scope until a real consumer surfaces: typed
175	errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`,
176	`Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand
177	status: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` (the "Refactor tracking" section).
178	
179	## per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`
180	
181	Independently of the Context+Params port — "the options_flags
182	extraction" — per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` fields are being
183	lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under `src/options_flags/`
184	so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer) exactly once. See
185	[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
186	for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
187	Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
188	per-subcommand status.
189	
190	## See also
191	
192	- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
193	  per-subcommand usage.
194	- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
195	  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
196	- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
197	  — leaf/bundle patterns for the options_flags extraction.
198	- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
199	  both refactors.
200	- `notes/chores/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
201	  refactor-tracking tables (currently
202	  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
203	  earlier are closed).
204

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores/chores-*.md`
and `notes/todo.md`.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand refactor status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and the
  `-R`/`-s` resolution stack — `find_workspace_root` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` / `resolve_repos`
  (the top-level entry combining the `-R PATH` override and
  the `-s code|bot|code,bot` role selection).

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `Scope` (a newtype over
  `Vec<Side>` — the `code`/`bot` role set) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` and which
compose `options_flags/` leaves: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` +
`notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## args → Context + Params

"The Context+Params port" — each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` is structured as
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, with an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0)
is the worked example; complete across all 12 current
subcommands as of 0.49.0, and the shape new subcommands
follow. Out of scope until a real consumer surfaces: typed
errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`,
`Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand
status: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` (the "Refactor tracking" section).

## per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of the Context+Params port — "the options_flags
extraction" — per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` fields are being
lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under `src/options_flags/`
so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for the options_flags extraction.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both refactors.
- `notes/chores/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  refactor-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).
